Class BloodUnit

java.lang.Object
BloodUnit
public class BloodUnit extends Object
Represents a blood unit in the blood bank management system. This class encapsulates all information related to a blood donation unit, including its unique identifier, blood type, donation and expiry dates, donor information, and current status. The expiry date is automatically calculated as 42 days from the donation date upon creation.

  • Constructor Details

    • BloodUnit

      public BloodUnit(String unitId, String bloodType, LocalDate donationDate, String donorId, String status)
      Constructs a BloodUnit with the specified parameters. This constructor initializes a new blood unit with the provided information. The expiry date is automatically calculated as 42 days from the donation date, as per blood bank regulations. This ensures that expiry dates are always consistent and cannot be set incorrectly.
      Parameters:
      unitId - the unique identifier for this blood unit (3-20 alphanumeric characters)
      bloodType - the blood type of this unit (e.g., "O+", "AB-")
      donationDate - the date this blood was donated
      donorId - the unique identifier of the donor (2-30 alphanumeric characters)
      status - the current status of the unit (e.g., "Available", "Quarantined", "Expired")

  • Method Details

    • getUnitId

      public String getUnitId()
      Returns the unique identifier of this blood unit.
      Returns:
      the unit ID (3-20 alphanumeric characters)

    • getBloodType

      public String getBloodType()
      Returns the blood type of this unit.
      Returns:
      the blood type (e.g., "O+", "AB-")

    • getDonationDate

      public LocalDate getDonationDate()
      Returns the date this blood was donated.
      Returns:
      the donation date

    • getExpiryDate

      public LocalDate getExpiryDate()
      Returns the expiration date of this blood unit. The expiry date is always 42 days after the donation date.
      Returns:
      the expiry date

    • getDonorId

      public String getDonorId()
      Returns the unique identifier of the donor who provided this unit.
      Returns:
      the donor ID (2-30 alphanumeric characters)

    • getStatus

      public String getStatus()
      Returns the current status of this blood unit.
      Returns:
      the status (e.g., "Available", "Quarantined", "Expired")

    • setUnitId

      public void setUnitId(String unitId)
      Sets the unique identifier for this blood unit.
      Parameters:
      unitId - the new unit ID (3-20 alphanumeric characters)

    • setBloodType

      public void setBloodType(String bloodType)
      Sets the blood type for this unit.
      Parameters:
      bloodType - the new blood type (e.g., "O+", "AB-")

    • setDonationDate

      public void setDonationDate(LocalDate donationDate)
      Sets the donation date for this unit and recalculates the expiry date. When the donation date is updated, the expiry date is automatically recalculated as 42 days from the new donation date to maintain consistency.
      Parameters:
      donationDate - the new donation date

    • setDonorId

      public void setDonorId(String donorId)
      Sets the donor ID for this blood unit.
      Parameters:
      donorId - the new donor ID (2-30 alphanumeric characters)

    • setStatus

      public void setStatus(String status)
      Sets the current status of this blood unit.
      Parameters:
      status - the new status (e.g., "Available", "Quarantined", "Expired")

    • toDisplayString

      public String toDisplayString()
      Returns a formatted string representation of this blood unit. The string includes all relevant information about the unit in a human-readable format suitable for display.
      Returns:
      a string containing all blood unit information
